_comment: Whisper ASR per-tensor tolerances for compare_tensors.py.
Regime: F32 GGUF + F32 KV cache + reference mel (ref-mel injected
via TRANSCRIBE_WHISPER_MEL_FROM_REF so enc.mel.in is bit-identical).
This is the correctness regime. Production variants (F16 model,
quants, F16 KV cache) are validated separately under Stage 5/6/7;
their drift does NOT flow into the numbers below.
Reference: transformers 5.6.1 (WhisperForConditionalGeneration) on
CPU, fp32 inference. Reference mel is the canonical WhisperFeature-
Extractor output (slaney filterbank + whisper_logmel compression).
Reference decoder is run as a single prefill pass over the canonical
multilingual prompt [<|sot|>, <|en|>, <|transcribe|>, <|notimestamps|>]
(token ids 50258, 50259, 50359, 50363) — same prompt for both jfk
and german cases since the dump harness does not change language
per audio file.
C++: ggml CPU backend, weights stored as F32 in GGUF (whisper-tiny
ships F32 upstream). Flash-attention is available on both paths but
is a no-op on numerics — encoder drift measured identical with
TRANSCRIBE_NO_FLASH=1, so this file does not name flash as a source.
Decoder runs through the KV-cached path by default: cross K/V are
precomputed once from the encoder output into the cross cache; self
K/V are written to the self cache as each token is processed. With
F32 cache the round-trip is lossless, so drift matches the non-
cached path within reproducible fp32 scheduler noise.
Tolerance recipe: uniform 1.5× worst-of-{jfk, german} observed drift,
rounded up in the last significant figure. Leaves enough headroom to
absorb fp32 reduction-order noise across machines but tight enough
that any per-tensor 5× regression fails validate.py. Zero-drift
tensors (GGUF reads + pure adds) are pinned at exact 0.0 — any
nonzero drift there indicates an unintended dtype conversion.
Escape hatches (these WILL widen the numbers below — measure
separately, do not conflate):
- TRANSCRIBE_WHISPER_NO_KV=1     : non-cached prefill per step
- --kv-type f16                  : F16 KV cache (~8× wider max)
Encoder drift profile (values from the worst of {jfk, german}):
Stem (conv stem) — clean.
enc.mel.in      0                 (ref-mel injection; when the
C++ mel frontend is exercised
instead the tolerance will
widen — measured separately)
enc.pos_emb     0                 (raw weight read from GGUF)
enc.conv1.out   2.22e-3 max,     (fp32 conv reduction order;
5.76e-5 mean      single matmul step; p99.9 is
8e-4 — a single-element outlier
drives the max)
enc.conv2.out   3.26e-3 max,     (stride-2 conv; slightly worse
1.49e-4 mean      than conv1 due to an extra
reduction axis)
enc.embed.out   equals conv2.out (add of a zero-drift pos_emb)
Transformer blocks — cumulative fp32 matmul drift.
enc.block.0.out 7.94e-3 max,     (~0.2% of peak signal 4.4)
5.48e-4 mean
enc.block.1.out 1.12e-2 max,     (~0.1% of peak signal 9.7)
8.45e-4 mean
enc.block.2.out 2.80e+0 max,     (peak signal spikes to 242 at
1.74e-3 mean      this layer; drift is ~1.2%
relative; mean stays tight)
enc.block.3.out 6.25e+0 max,     (peak 265; ~2.4% relative; mean
4.36e-3 mean      compounds as expected)
enc.final       1.46e+0 max,     (post final LN; peak 17.4;
4.02e-3 mean      ~8% relative in worst element
because LN amplifies drift on
the tensor whose inputs had
~242 outliers)
Decoder drift profile (values from the worst of {jfk, german}, F32
KV-cached path on whisper-tiny-F32.gguf, prompt pass seq_len=4):
Embedding stage — clean.
dec.token_emb   0                 (ggml_get_rows on the F32 GGUF
embedding table; bit-identical)
dec.pos_emb     0                 (ggml_get_rows on the F32 GGUF
positional embedding table)
dec.embed_sum   0                 (add of two zero-drift tensors)
Transformer blocks — cumulative fp32 matmul drift. The F32 cache
round-trip is lossless; drift matches non-cached prefill within
fp32 scheduler noise. max_abs on blocks 1/2/3 is dominated by one
outlier activation at pos=1 feat=73 on the german case (signal
magnitude ~81; drift ~3e-2 = 0.04% relative) — same class of
compute-path-specific outlier already seen on enc.block.2/3.
dec.block.0.out 6.15e-3 max,     (~0.06% of peak signal ~10)
5.95e-4 mean
dec.block.1.out 2.98e-2 max,     (~0.04% of peak ~81)
8.55e-4 mean
dec.block.2.out 2.91e-2 max,     (~0.04% of peak ~82)
1.08e-3 mean
dec.block.3.out 2.86e-2 max,     (~0.04% of peak ~77)
1.81e-3 mean
dec.out_before_head
1.35e+0 max,     (post final LN; peak ~229;
3.17e-2 mean      ~0.6% relative)
dec.logits_raw  1.79e-1 max,     (vocab projection; peak ~37;
3.43e-2 mean      ~0.5% relative)
dec.logits      7.75e-2 max,     (log_softmax; ~0.2% on the
1.05e-2 mean      -35..0 range)
Dominant drift mechanism: cumulative fp32 matmul reduction-order
divergence between ggml CPU kernels and torch.nn.Linear's BLAS. No
layer-norm eps mismatch (both 1e-5), no attention-path divergence
(flash on/off identical), no layout bug (first/last elements match
sign and magnitude on every tensor; drift is distributed, not
structural). Typical p99.9 drift per tensor is 1-2 orders of
magnitude below the reported max_abs.
Attenuation pattern: GROWING through both encoder and decoder —
expected for compounding fp32 error. Decoder blocks plateau after
block 1 because peak signal plateaus (~77-82); encoder's block 2/3
jump is driven by whisper-internal outlier activations at those
layers (max signal ~242).
_suspects: enc.block.2.out, enc.block.3.out, enc.final. These carry
max_abs tolerances exceeding 5% of peak signal. Named mechanism in
each case is whisper-internal outlier activations combined with
fp32 reduction-order drift; mean_abs stays well under 0.2% relative
on every one. Accepted as the honest drift profile for fp32 CPU
compute against the transformers reference; the Stage 6 ref-dtype
WER gate is the outer safety net.
No decoder _suspects. Every dec.* tensor's max_abs is under 1% of
its peak signal magnitude (worst is dec.out_before_head at ~0.6%).
Mid-generation coverage:
dec.logits_raw.gen20  The logits produced by the decoder's 20th
autoregressive step — i.e. pass the KV-
cached decoder 20 tokens beyond the prompt
prefix and capture the pre-argmax logits
that would predict the 21st generated
token. This is the only tensor-level test
of the n_past>0 KV cache read/write path;
the dec.* tensors above only cover the
prompt pass (n_past=0). Drift (~1.3e-1
max, ~0.1 mean on JFK; smaller on German)
reflects compounded fp32 error across 20
autoregressive steps + 4 transformer
blocks + cross-attention. C++ and ref both
run their own greedy loops matching the
run() driver's suppress_tokens / begin-
suppress policy, so argmax paths stay
aligned through step 20.
